hypothesis: Under DAC-relevant humid conditions (298 K, 0.4 mbar CO2, 40% RH), amine-based polymer sorbents with a secondary-to-primary amine ratio ≥1.5 will exhibit ≥20% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake than otherwise identical polymers with a ratio ≤0.5 when confined in the same porous support at equal total amine density.
